chapter: Chapter: Weavers, Iron Smelters and Factory Owners

questions.question: Why were Indian textiles in great demand in the international market?

options.A: They were cheap and poorly made
options.B: They were colorful, fine and durable
options.D: They were made only for export

questions.explanation: Indian cotton textiles were famous for their fine quality, colors and durability.

questions.question: Which European company first began trading Indian textiles on a large scale?
questions.explanation: The English East India Company traded Indian textiles widely in international markets.

questions.question: What problem did Indian weavers face after the Industrial Revolution?
questions.explanation: Machine-made cloth from British factories created strong competition for Indian weavers.

questions.question: What role did the East India Company play in the textile trade?
questions.explanation: The East India Company controlled production, prices and trade of textiles.

questions.question: Why were many Indian weavers forced to borrow money from traders?

options.B: To buy raw materials and survive low wages

questions.explanation: Weavers borrowed money to buy raw materials and manage economic hardships.

questions.question: Which change affected traditional iron smelters during British rule?

options.B: Decline due to forest laws and imported iron

questions.explanation: British forest laws and imported iron caused the decline of traditional iron smelting.

questions.question: Why did British forest laws affect iron smelters?

options.A: They restricted access to wood used as fuel

questions.explanation: Iron smelters needed wood for fuel, but forest laws restricted access to forests.

questions.question: Which invention increased textile production in factories?
questions.explanation: Power looms increased cloth production in textile factories.

questions.question: Why did factory production increase during the Industrial Revolution?
questions.explanation: Machines increased production speed and efficiency in factories.

questions.question: Which city became a major textile mill centre in India?
questions.explanation: Bombay developed as a major textile mill centre.

questions.question: Who established many early Indian textile mills?
questions.explanation: Indian entrepreneurs played an important role in establishing textile mills.

questions.question: Why did handloom weaving decline during British rule?

options.B: Due to competition from machine-made cloth

questions.explanation: Machine-made cloth from Britain was cheaper and produced faster.

questions.question: Which industry replaced many traditional weaving activities?
questions.explanation: Factory-based textile production replaced many traditional weaving activities.

questions.question: What was one advantage of factory production?
questions.explanation: Factories could produce goods faster using machines.

questions.question: Why did Indian entrepreneurs start textile mills?
questions.explanation: Indian entrepreneurs started mills to compete with imported cloth.

questions.question: Which resource was essential for iron smelting?
questions.explanation: Iron smelting required iron ore and wood as fuel.

questions.question: Which factor caused the decline of iron smelting in India?
questions.explanation: Imported iron and restrictions on forest use led to the decline of iron smelting.

questions.question: Which group benefited most from factory industries?
questions.explanation: Factory owners benefited from industrial production and profits.

questions.question: What was the main difference between handloom weaving and factory production?
questions.explanation: Factories used machines while handloom weaving was done manually.

questions.question: Which change increased the production of cloth in factories?
questions.explanation: Power looms increased cloth production in factories.

questions.question: Which activity did traditional weavers perform before factories?
questions.explanation: Traditional weavers produced cloth using handlooms.

questions.question: Which factor helped Indian entrepreneurs establish textile mills?
questions.explanation: Cotton supply and labour availability helped establish textile mills.

questions.question: Which industry was most affected by British machine-made goods?
questions.explanation: Handloom weaving suffered due to competition from machine-made cloth.

questions.question: Which industrial change transformed cloth production?
questions.explanation: Machines transformed cloth production during industrialization.

questions.question: What was the long-term impact of factory industries in India?
questions.explanation: Factory industries contributed to the growth of industrial production.
